m1a1_transmission.answer:
### BLUF
**Five serial-numbered M1A1s are at elevated risk for transmission failure: USMC-M1A1-40079, -40032, -40067, -40037, and -40017, all with open “transmission slipping under load” work orders within the last 90 days; TM 9-2350-264-10 flags TS-2/TS-4 as impending clutch pack failure requiring immediate Q-1 service.**

### Findings (per-node)
- **MCLB Albany — GCSS-MC:** Open transmission-related work orders in the last 90 days for **USMC-M1A1-40079** (opened **45d** ago, 3d Bn 2d Marines), **USMC-M1A1-40032** (**83d**, CLB-2), **USMC-M1A1-40067** (**65d**, 1st Bn 6th Marines), **USMC-M1A1-40037** (**25d**, CLB-2), and **USMC-M1A1-40017** (**31d**, CLB-2); all are **OPEN** with the fault description **“Transmission slipping under load.”**
- **MCB Lejeune — ICM:** No direct M1A1 transmission parts record was retrieved; however, **1st Bn 6th Marines** appears in the lateral-transfer/parts data, which is relevant for support coordination if **USMC-M1A1-40067** requires expedited parts or cross-leveling.
- **MCB Quantico — TM Library:** **TM 9-2350-264-10 (M1A1 Transmission Service Limits)** states **TS-2 / TS-4** diagnostic codes indicate **impending clutch pack failure** and require **immediate Q-1 service**.

### Federated recommendation
1. **For the Marine units holding the affected tanks** — prioritize **Q-1 inspection and transmission diagnostics** on **USMC-M1A1-40079, -40032, -40067, -40037, and -40017** before the pre-deployment readiness call; treat any **TS-2/TS-4** findings as immediate maintenance action.
2. **For CLB-2 and 1st Bn 6th Marines** — stage recovery/maintenance windows for **USMC-M1A1-40032, -40037, -40017, and -40067** and pre-coordinate transmission/clutch-pack support against **TM 9-2350-264-10** service limits.
3. **For the depot maintenance planner at MCLB Albany** — flag these five serials as the current transmission-risk set from the last 90 days and track them as a readiness priority until the open work orders are closed or the vehicles pass Q-1 service.

### Compute-at-data posture
This answer was composed entirely from per-node retrieval summaries; no source data moved off **MCLB Albany**, **MCB Lejeune**, or **MCB Quantico**. The federated process respected **ICD 503** enclave handling and **DCSA spillage controls**, using compute-at-data so only minimal, non-source summaries crossed the wire.

prc117_battery.answer:
### BLUF
**Within 14 days, MAGTF Logistics Group and 2d Bn 8th Marines are projected to run out of BB-2590 batteries for AN/PRC-117G manpacks; CLB-2 can lateral support both, and 1st Bn 6th Marines is not at risk inside the 14-day window.**

### Findings (per-node)
- **MCB Lejeune (ICM):** MAGTF Logistics Group holds **3x BB-2590 Battery, AN/PRC-117G (NSN 5945-01-553-1212)** with **7d days-to-zero**; **2d Bn 8th Marines** holds **13x** and also **8x BB-2590 Battery, AN/PRC-117G (NSN 5945-01-553-1212)**, both with **7d days-to-zero**. Lateral feasibility is **HIGH from CLB-2 stock** for each. **1st Bn 6th Marines** holds **5x** of the same NSN with **21d days-to-zero**, so it is outside the 14-day risk window.
- **MCLB Albany (GCSS-MC):** No BB-2590 / AN/PRC-117G battery shortages or lateral-transfer records were returned in the retrieval set; only unrelated M1A1 work orders were present, including **USMC-M1A1-40079**, **USMC-M1A1-40059**, **USMC-M1A1-40032**, **USMC-M1A1-40067**, and **USMC-M1A1-40037**.

### Federated recommendation
1. **Prioritize lateral transfer from CLB-2 stock to MAGTF Logistics Group** for **NSN 5945-01-553-1212** to cover the **7-day** projected depletion.
2. **Issue lateral support to 2d Bn 8th Marines** for the same **BB-2590 / AN/PRC-117G** NSN, since both holdings reported at **7d days-to-zero** are inside the 14-day window.
3. **Defer action for 1st Bn 6th Marines** unless burn rate changes, because its **21d days-to-zero** does not indicate an imminent outage.

### Compute-at-data posture
This answer was composed entirely from per-node retrieval summaries; no source records were moved or centrally aggregated. The workflow stayed within the compute-at-data model and respects **ICD-503 enclave boundaries** and **DCSA spillage controls**, with only minimal, non-source summaries crossing the wire.

jltv_brake_recall.answer:
### BLUF
**No JLTV serials are identified in the provided federated retrievals as having used recalled brake-caliper lot LRC-2024-3318 in the last 18 months; the only affected item surfaced is the recalled JLTV brake-caliper lot itself, with a production window of Jul-2024 through Sep-2024.**

### Findings (per-node)
- **MCLB Albany — GCSS-MC:** No JLTV brake-caliper lot matches were returned. The retrieved work orders were for **M1A1** serials only: **USMC-M1A1-40079**, **USMC-M1A1-40059**, and **USMC-M1A1-40032**.
- **MCB Quantico — TM Library:** The enclave retrieval identified **MWO/REC 2024-3318** for the **JLTV Brake Caliper Lot Recall**, affecting **lot LRC-2024-3318** and the production window **Jul-2024 through Sep-2024**; no JLTV serial numbers were included in the returned summary.
- **MCB Lejeune — ICM lateral-transfer + parts records:** No retrieval summary was provided from this node, so no JLTV serials or NSNs could be confirmed from Lejeune in the federated result.

### Federated recommendation
1. **MCLB Albany maintenance control** should cross-check any JLTVs in the local fleet against **lot LRC-2024-3318** for the **Jul-2024 to Sep-2024** production window and flag any serials for immediate inspection.
2. **MCB Lejeune ICM parts/accountability** should query its lateral-transfer and parts records for the same recalled lot and identify any JLTV serials that received calipers from **LRC-2024-3318** within the last **18 months**.
3. **Quantico TM/maintenance authority** should publish the recall applicability guidance for **MWO/REC 2024-3318** to the affected depot and unit maintenance chiefs so they can isolate any JLTVs tied to the recalled lot.

### Compute-at-data posture
This answer was composed strictly from per-node retrieval summaries; no source maintenance corpus was moved into a central index or copied across enclaves. The Quantico material remained inside the **ICD 503** enclave, and the federated workflow respected **DCSA spillage controls** by returning only minimal, non-exfiltrated findings needed to answer the commander’s question.

m240_barrel_life.answer:
### BLUF
**No M240B barrels are identified in the federated retrievals as having exceeded 80% of the TM threshold; the TM at Quantico sets the 80% inspection point at 12,000 rounds on a 15,000-round quick-change barrel, but no node returned barrel serials or round counts to show any barrel is at or above that mark.**

### Findings (per-node)
- **MCB Quantico — TM Library (CUI / ICD 503 enclave):** TM 9-1005-313-10 publishes the M240B quick-change barrel threshold as **15,000 rounds**, with **bore inspection at 12,000 rounds (80%)**. No barrel serials, unit IDs, or actual round counts were returned in the retrieval.
- **MCB Lejeune — ICM (UNCLASSIFIED // FOUO):** The only M240-related supply record is **NSN 1005-01-440-1010, M240 Barrel Assy, Quick-Change**, with **6 on hand** at **MAGTF Logistics Group** and **projected days-to-zero: 60d**. No round-count work orders or barrel serials were returned.
- **MCLB Albany — GCSS-MC (UNCLASSIFIED):** Retrieved work orders concern **M1A1** systems only (e.g., **USMC-M1A1-40079**, **USMC-M1A1-40059**, **USMC-M1A1-40032**); no M240B barrel work orders or round-count records were present.

### Federated recommendation
1. **Have the armorer at MAGTF Logistics Group** pull the **6x NSN 1005-01-440-1010 M240 Barrel Assy, Quick-Change** and match each barrel’s serial to its round-count log; flag any barrel at **12,000 rounds or more** for bore inspection per **TM 9-1005-313-10**.
2. **Task the supported unit armorer / maintenance chief** to reconcile any M240B barrel work orders against the TM threshold and annotate the barrel’s current location, since the federated data did not include barrel serials or current round counts.
3. **If any barrel is at or above 12,000 rounds,** move it to inspection/rotation using the **MAGTF Logistics Group** stock first, preserving serviceable barrels for the highest-demand Marine unit.

### Compute-at-data posture
This answer was composed entirely from per-node retrieval summaries; no source data moved off **MCLB Albany**, **MCB Lejeune**, or the **MCB Quantico ICD 503 enclave**. The TM content remained at Quantico under **ICD 503** and **DCSA spillage controls**, and only the minimal federated summaries crossed the wire for analysis.

mtvr_tire_lateral.answer:
### BLUF
**The lowest-cost lateral-transfer plan is to source all 12 MTVR 16R20 tires from a single high-feasibility holder at MCB Lejeune—preferably 8th ESB’s 12 tires of NSN 2610-01-422-7100—because it satisfies the full requirement with one transfer and avoids split-move handling costs.**

### Findings (per-node)
- **MCLB Albany:** GCSS-MC shows open MTVR work orders on serials **USMC-MTVR-30011, -30039, -30004, -30006, and -30035** (caliper sticking, transmission overheat, CAN bus dropout, transmission slipping, hydraulic line weep). These indicate maintenance demand but do **not** provide a tire source for the exercise window.
- **MCB Lejeune:** ICM shows multiple holders of **MTVR 16R20 Tire, NSN 2610-01-422-7100** with lateral feasibility **HIGH**. The best single-source match is **8th ESB holding 12x** of the exact NSN, which exactly meets the battalion requirement of 12 tires. Other available pools include **CLB-2 (10x)**, **2d Bn 8th Marines (16x and 9x entries)**, and **2d Tank Bn (legacy) (3x)**.
- **MCB Quantico:** No tire inventory was retrieved from the NSN-tagged technical-manual library; no source quantities or lateral candidates for **NSN 2610-01-422-7100** were returned.

### Federated recommendation
1. **Direct the lateral transfer from 8th ESB at MCB Lejeune to the requesting battalion** for **12x MTVR 16R20 Tire, NSN 2610-01-422-7100**; this is the cheapest plan because it is a single-source, exact-quantity move.
2. **If 8th ESB cannot release all 12, backfill from CLB-2 at Lejeune** with **10x NSN 2610-01-422-7100**, then source the remaining **2x** from **2d Tank Bn (legacy)** or **2d Bn 8th Marines** to complete the set.
3. **Have the Marine planner prioritize the exact NSN match only** and avoid piecemeal sourcing from Albany maintenance assets, since Albany’s retrieved records are repair actions, not tire stock.

### Compute-at-data posture
This answer was composed from per-node retrieval summaries only; no source data moved off **MCLB Albany, MCB Lejeune, or MCB Quantico**. The federation respected **ICD 503** enclave boundaries and DCSA-style spillage controls by returning only the minimal necessary retrieval results, not underlying records or raw databases.
